heading: Dividends and surplus funds of reserve banks; transfer for fiscal year 2000
label: 289 Dividends and surplus funds of reserve banks; transfer for fiscal year 2000

(a)(1)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally. L. 114–94, § 32203(a)(1)Subsec. (a)(1)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 114–94, § 32203(a)(2)Subsec. (a)(3). , added par. (3).Pub. L. 114–94, § 322021999—Subsec. (a)(3). , struck out heading and text of par. (3). Text read as follows: “During fiscal years 1997 and 1998, any amount in the surplus fund of any Federal reserve bank in excess of the amount equal to 3 percent of the total paid-in capital and surplus of the member banks of such bank shall be transferred to the Board for transfer to the Secretary of the Treasury for deposit in the general fund of the Treasury.”Pub. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “After all necessary expenses of a Federal reserve bank shall have been paid or provided for, the stockholders shall be entitled to receive an annual dividend of 6 per centum on the paid-in capital stock, which dividend shall be cumulative. After the aforesaid dividend claims have been fully met, the net earnings shall be paid into the surplus fund of the Federal reserve bank.”Pub. children.heading: Dividends and surplus funds of reserve banks
children.label: a Dividends and surplus funds of reserve banks
I
children.text: the rate equal to the high yield of the 10-year Treasury note auctioned at the last auction held prior to the payment of such dividend; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a stockholder with total consolidated assets of $10,000,000,000 or less, 6 percent.

children.heading: Dividend cumulative
children.label: B Dividend cumulative
children.text: The entitlement to dividends under subparagraph (A) shall be cumulative.

children.heading: Inflation adjustment
children.label: C Inflation adjustment
children.text: The Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System shall annually adjust the dollar amounts of total consolidated assets specified under subparagraph (A) to reflect the change in the Gross Domestic Product Price Index, published by the Bureau of Economic Analysis.

children.heading: Deposit of net earnings in surplus fund
children.label: 2 Deposit of net earnings in surplus fund
children.text: That portion of net earnings of each Federal reserve bank which remains after dividend claims under paragraph (1)(A) have been fully met shall be deposited in the surplus fund of the bank.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The aggregate amount of the surplus funds of the Federal reserve banks may not exceed $6,825,000,000.

children.heading: Transfer to the general fund
children.label: B Transfer to the general fund
children.text: Any amounts of the surplus funds of the Federal reserve banks that exceed, or would exceed, the limitation under subparagraph (A) shall be transferred to the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System for transfer to the Secretary of the Treasury for deposit in the general fund of the Treasury.

children.heading: Transfer for fiscal year 20001See Codification note below.1
children.label: b Transfer for fiscal year 20001See Codification note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Federal reserve banks shall transfer from the surplus funds of such banks to the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System for transfer to the Secretary of the Treasury for deposit in the general fund of the Treasury, a total amount of $3,752,000,000 in fiscal year 2000.

children.heading: Allocated by Fed
children.label: 2 Allocated by Fed
children.text: Of the total amount required to be paid by the Federal reserve banks under paragraph (1) for fiscal year 2000, the Board shall determine the amount each such bank shall pay in such fiscal year.

children.heading: Replenishment of surplus fund prohibited
children.label: 3 Replenishment of surplus fund prohibited
children.text: During fiscal year 2000, no Federal reserve bank may replenish such bank’s surplus fund by the amount of any transfer by such bank under paragraph (1).
